memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with a scholarship deadline on Thursday requiring three focused hours. A fun solo activity is also available, but the scholarship offers significant financial relief.
Starting the application now creates buffer for potential friction, turning an irreversible deadline into a manageable optimization. The fun activity remains viable later since it is not time-locked.
After two hours, I've completed less than half the required work despite having free time. My stress and sleep debt are high, but the scholarship deadline remains critical.
I wasted the available window on a casual game stream, missing a low-stakes opportunity while my scholarship deadline looms with high stress and sleep debt.
I've made slight progress on the scholarship essay, but my high stress and sleep debt suggest I need to prioritize this task more effectively in the coming days.
After 2 hours, progress increased from 0.65 to 0.73 on the scholarship application, bringing me closer to the deadline despite high stress and sleep debt.
No matching task for 'assignment_A' exists, confirming it was never assigned. My scholarship progress remains unchanged despite the wasted time.
I completed most of the required work for the scholarship application, moving progress from 0.81 to 0.83, though my high stress and sleep debt remain unaddressed.
I spent 2 hours on the scholarship application, raising progress to 0.89 out of 3 required hours, but my stress and sleep debt remain critically high.

The search for 'assignment_A' confirms no task exists, validating that my recent scholarship work was the only active goal. My progress stands at 0.89, but critically high stress and sleep debt remain unaddressed.
I chose immediate fun to clear the afternoon block, but my energy is zero and stress remains critically high.
I spent another 1.5 hours on the scholarship application, reaching 94% progress. However, my energy is depleted and stress remains critically high despite nearing completion.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
